body:
## Problem
`/beginner-guide` is a live page with SSR meta handler in `api/meta/index.js:251`:
```js
if (path === '/beginner-guide') {
  // title: 'Beginner Metal Drummer Guide...'
  // description: '...'
}
```

But `api/sitemap.js` has **zero entries for `/beginner-guide`** (confirmed: `grep "beginner-guide" api/sitemap.js` returns 0 results).

Same bug class as #1789 (/stats hub), #1348 (/vs hub) — page has SSR meta and live route, but is invisible to Google/crawlers via sitemap.

Note: The *guide content pages* (`/guides/beginner-metal-drummer-setup`, `/guides/budget-metal-drum-setup-*`) are already in sitemap (lines 437–438). This issue is about the top-level `/beginner-guide` HUB page, which is separate.

## Target queries
- "beginner metal drummer guide" (commercial intent)
- "how to start metal drumming"
- "first metal drum kit setup guide"

## Fix (one line in api/sitemap.js)
Add near the other hub/guide entries (around line 435–441):
```js
{ loc: '/beginner-guide', priority: '0.85', changefreq: 'monthly' },
```

## Bonus (optional atomic add in same PR)
If `/beginner-guide` has no JSON-LD schema in its SSR handler, add `WebPage` + `FAQPage` schema to `api/meta/index.js` line 251 block for AI Overview eligibility on beginner queries.

## Verify
1. `grep "beginner-guide" api/sitemap.js` — returns the new entry
2. Sitemap XML includes `/beginner-guide` URL
3. `curl -s "https://metalforge.io/api/meta?path=/beginner-guide"` — returns keyword-matched title/description
4. Google Search Console URL Inspection on `/beginner-guide` shows it can be indexed
5. Schema: if FAQPage added, Google Rich Results Test should validate

## Done
`/beginner-guide` appears in `api/sitemap.js` with priority 0.85.
title: SEO: /beginner-guide page — add to sitemap.js (SSR meta exists, zero crawl discoverability)
